pattern: Greedily closing each qualifying substring at its earliest possible end leaves the most room for later ones.
hints: A valid piece needs length at least four and identical first and last letters.
Scan left to right; for the current letter, the earliest start that makes a length-four-or-more piece is the first time you saw that letter in the open region.
When you can close such a piece, take it, jump past its end, and forget all earlier letter positions before continuing.

edge_inputs.description: matching endpoints but only length three

stress.description: long strings from a tiny alphabet to create many candidates
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    alphabet = 'abcd'
    n = rng.randint(1, max(1, size))
    word = ''.join(rng.choice(alphabet) for _ in range(n))
    return [word]
stress.note: small alphabet maximizes overlapping valid substrings
